metadata.title: Deploy a Keyword Spotter: Find the Layer That Leaked Accuracy

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. The model was 94 percent accurate in Python. After the toolchain got done with it, it was 91 percent — and the 3 percent vanished in a step nobody watched.

props.command: claude "write deploy_runner.py: convert a keras model to TFLite int8, run the TFLite interpreter on a val set, compare to the float model, and bisect which layer/op introduced the largest output divergence."
props.segment: Deploy a Keyword Spotter: Find the Layer That Leaked Accuracy
output: Divergence peak: layer conv2d_3 (unsupported op fusion)

beats.narration_text: The book's six-step deployment pipeline. Our deploy runner automates steps three through five — optimize, compile, verify — and flags the dominant failure mode at each gate.

beats.act: MANIM — Pipeline: accuracy dropping at each stage
beats.narration_text: Six stages in the deployment pipeline. Keras float source: 94 percent. Export to SavedModel: 94 percent. Convert to TFLite float: 93.5 percent — half a percent already gone. Convert to TFLite INT8: 91 percent. The 3 percent gap lives inside the conversion. Nobody checked at each hop.

beats.act: MANIM — Bisection: divergence bars spike at failing op
beats.narration_text: Run the bisection. Feed the same input through the float model and the INT8 model. At each layer, measure divergence — the L2 distance between outputs. The first few layers are clean. Then one bar shoots up. That is the leaking operation — an unsupported op fusion that falls back to a non-quantized CPU kernel.

beats.act: MANIM — Lesson: check at every hop
beats.narration_text: The lesson: check at every hop. A green end-to-end test hides silent failures. The bisection finds them. Any unsupported op that falls back to the CPU is a candidate for the divergence — it mixes precision, introducing error that accumulates downstream.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write deploy_runner.py — convert a Keras model to TFLite INT8, run the TFLite interpreter on a validation set, compare to the float model, and bisect which layer or op introduced the largest output divergence. This is the bisection test that finds silent failures. Add a per-op CMSIS-NN support check — any unsupported op that falls back to CPU is a candidate for the divergence.

props.command: claude "write deploy_runner.py: convert a keras model to TFLite int8, run the TFLite interpreter on a val set, compare to the float model, and bisect which layer/op introduced the largest output divergence."
